etymology.description: semantic 大 (dà, "big; great") + phonetic 圭 (guī, "jade tablet") -> a star constellation
etymology.interpretation: The character 奎 (kuí, "a star constellation") combines 大 (dà, "big") as the semantic component, indicating something grand like a constellation, with 圭 (guī, "jade tablet") as the phonetic component, providing the sound. While 圭 originally depicted ritual axes, it was repurposed here only for its phonetic value to express the name of this significant constellation.

examples.chinese: 奎星是中华文化中的吉祥象征(奎星是中華文化中的吉祥象徵)。
examples.pinyin: Kuí xīng shì Zhōnghuá wénhuà zhōng de jíxiáng xiàngzhēng.
examples.english: The star constellation Kuí is an auspicious symbol in Chinese culture.

examples.chinese: 在古代(在古代)，人们常常观测奎星的位置(人們常常觀測奎星的位置)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, rénmen chángcháng guāncè kuí xīng de wèizhì.
examples.english: In ancient times, people often observed the position of the star constellation Kuí.

examples.chinese: 奎星被视为智慧与才能的象征(奎星被視為智慧與才能的象徵)。
examples.pinyin: Kuí xīng bèi shì wéi zhìhuì yǔ cáinéng de xiàngzhēng.
examples.english: The star constellation Kuí is regarded as a symbol of wisdom and talent.
